title: Rosa Parks Trial
personalObjective: To overcome my fear of presenting in front of the entire class and develop acting and historical empathy skills by portraying Claudette Colvin in a recreation of the Rosa Parks trial.
personalChallenge: I was afraid of not doing well or making mistakes, as public speaking is something that affects me quite a bit.

investigation.title: Investigation
investigation.content: In History class, we are working on civil rights in the United States. They explained Claudette Colvin's case in class, and then I had to search for more information on Google. There was quite a bit of content since her case is well-known, unlike other plaintiffs like Susie McDonald, about whom there isn't as much material.\n\nClaudette Colvin was a 15-year-old teenager, emotional and with firm principles. I thought she fit me very well, which is why I raised my hand when the teacher mentioned this character.

preparation.title: Preparation and Rehearsal
preparation.content:
As soon as I knew my role, I started working on the character. I searched for information and wrote my intervention about how Claudette and her classmates were returning from school on the bus, how she sat by the window, and how she refused to get up when the driver ordered her to.

I decided I wasn't going to say everything literally because it's better to act naturally. The most important thing for me was to understand my role and feel what she went through; from there, I could improvise a bit.

Although I find it difficult to act in public, I asked myself: 'What did Claudette feel at that moment? How unjust was everything that was happening in Montgomery in those years?'

action.title: Action
action.content: On October 3rd, we held the 'Rosa Parks Trial'. On the day of the trial, they gave us 30 minutes to review. When it started, they called the plaintiffs and I was second.\n\nI was very nervous, but I really liked the performance we were doing because everyone had prepared very well. Scared but determined, I stood up and started speaking, explaining my experience as Claudette Colvin on the bus.

demonstration.title: Demonstration
demonstration.content: During the trial, I portrayed Claudette Colvin narrating how my classmates and I were returning from school on the bus, how I sat by the window and refused to get up when the driver ordered me to. The recreation was very emotional and all participants prepared excellently.\n\nThe experience allowed me to put myself in the shoes of someone who experienced racial segregation firsthand.

reflection.title: Reflection
reflection.content:
Being Claudette provoked many emotions in me, trying to feel what she would feel at that moment. Yes, she is a teenager, and that's why we are similar, but I wouldn't rebel as much as she did. I was surprised by her attitude on the bus, just like Rosa Parks' attitude.

In the end, the recreation made me understand what was really happening in the US regarding segregation and discrimination against people of color, and how unjust everything truly was. I can't imagine what I could do in the place of these people. But surely, I would feel very powerless and sad.

Overall, I really enjoyed it and I think it opened my eyes to what public presentations are like, and that even though you're scared, you have to overcome it and have a good time. The point was to recreate the Rosa Parks trial, and I think it turned out quite well.
